verdict.answer_first: B states the rule in sentence 1: "never change the meaning of an existing contract; only add to it"; A opens with vague positioning: "battle-tested plan" before the usable principle.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits cleaner to one default: "default to URL path versioning" and "Avoid" query params; A gives a recommendation but dilutes it with "strong alternative" and extra follow-up branches.
verdict.fabrication: A invents context not in the source: "This dir (`/private/tmp`) isn't a repo". B: none.
verdict.actionability: A has more executable safeguards: "OpenAPI spec + CI breaking-change linter", "Deprecation/Sunset headers", "6–12 month deprecation policy", but B is cleaner and less noisy.
